69fe6774e2442def495d85aa.question: One criticism of this school of thought entails a "sheriff scenario” and was posited by H.J. McCloskey. An argument analogising a drowning child to Bangladeshi refugees was put forward by a thinker from this school of thought and appears in the essay “Famine, Affluence and Morality”. Henry Sidgwick was an early proponent of this school of thought and described this school’s “paradox of (*) hedonism” in one work. In his book Principles, R.M. Hare uses this school of thought’s “act” and “rule” theories to explain the outcomes of when people “take on the role of the ideal observer”. Jeremy Bentham was considered the modern father of this school of thought. For 10 points, identify this school of thought that advocates maximising happiness for the greatest number of people.
69fe6774e2442def495d85aa.question_sanitized: One criticism of this school of thought entails a "sheriff scenario" and was posited by H.J. McCloskey. An argument analogising a drowning child to Bangladeshi refugees was put forward by a thinker from this school of thought and appears in the essay "Famine, Affluence and Morality". Henry Sidgwick was an early proponent of this school of thought and described this school's "paradox of (*) hedonism" in one work. In his book Principles, R.M. Hare uses this school of thought's "act" and "rule" theories to explain the outcomes of when people "take on the role of the ideal observer". Jeremy Bentham was considered the modern father of this school of thought. For 10 points, identify this school of thought that advocates maximising happiness for the greatest number of people.
69fe6774e2442def495d85aa.answer: utilitarianism [accept ideal utilitarianism, accept act utilitarianism or rule utilitarianism, accept classical utilitarianism]
69fe6774e2442def495d85aa.answer_sanitized: utilitarianism [accept ideal utilitarianism, accept act utilitarianism or rule utilitarianism, accept classical utilitarianism]

69fe6774e2442def495d85be.leadin: Followers of this thinker coined the term “haecceity” (“heck-SEE-i-tee”), which denotes a property that makes a thing that particular thing. For 10 points each:
69fe6774e2442def495d85be.leadin_sanitized: Followers of this thinker coined the term "haecceity" ("heck-SEE-i-tee"), which denotes a property that makes a thing that particular thing. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this priest and philosopher who created the doctrine of “univocity of being”, the belief that existence is the most abstract concept we can comprehend. This thinker’s principal doctrines are set out in his commentaries on the Sentences of Peter Lombard.

Scotus was a follower of this philosophical movement, of which Anselm of Canterbury was called the “Father of”. Most popular in the 12-14th centuries, this movement sought to reconcile classical and ancient philosophy and Catholic theology through precise logic.

Another influential scholasticist is this 13th century theologian who argued that God is the natural source of reason and faith and who synthesized Aristotelian philosophy with Catholic thought in his Summa Theologica.
parts_sanitized: Name this priest and philosopher who created the doctrine of "univocity of being", the belief that existence is the most abstract concept we can comprehend. This thinker's principal doctrines are set out in his commentaries on the Sentences of Peter Lombard.

Scotus was a follower of this philosophical movement, of which Anselm of Canterbury was called the "Father of". Most popular in the 12-14th centuries, this movement sought to reconcile classical and ancient philosophy and Catholic theology through precise logic.

Another influential scholasticist is this 13th century theologian who argued that God is the natural source of reason and faith and who synthesized Aristotelian philosophy with Catholic thought in his Summa Theologica.
answers: Saint Thomas Aquinas [or Tommaso d’Aquino; prompt on Saint Thomas or Santo Tommaso]
answers_sanitized: Saint Thomas Aquinas [or Tommaso d'Aquino; prompt on Saint Thomas or Santo Tommaso]

69fe677ae2442def495d85f9.question: A character in this play mocks a man’s description of an abstract painting by describing it as having “a quietly noisy relaxed intensity.” In this play, a man accuses another character of wanting to perform genetic engineering on chromosomes and repeatedly misidentifies him as a history teacher. A fifty-two year old character described as “ample, but not fleshy” makes up a story about an (*) imagined son vomiting in this play’s “Walpurgisnacht” section. A woman is revealed to have had a “hysterical pregnancy” in this play before her husband is forced to play “hump the hostess.” At this play’s beginning, a woman imitates Bette Davis by saying “what a dump!” before the arrival of Nick and Honey. For 10 points, name this play about George and Martha by Edward Albee.
69fe677ae2442def495d85f9.question_sanitized: A character in this play mocks a man's description of an abstract painting by describing it as having "a quietly noisy relaxed intensity." In this play, a man accuses another character of wanting to perform genetic engineering on chromosomes and repeatedly misidentifies him as a history teacher. A fifty-two year old character described as "ample, but not fleshy" makes up a story about an (*) imagined son vomiting in this play's "Walpurgisnacht" section. A woman is revealed to have had a "hysterical pregnancy" in this play before her husband is forced to play "hump the hostess." At this play's beginning, a woman imitates Bette Davis by saying "what a dump!" before the arrival of Nick and Honey. For 10 points, name this play about George and Martha by Edward Albee.

69fe677ae2442def495d8607.question: As part of her work on artificial intelligence, Margaret Masterman was the first to introduce this philosopher’s work into computational linguistics. Colour-coded notebooks of lectures by this philosopher were later published as the Blue and Brown Books. In a 1982 work, another philosopher explained a “sceptical problem” introduced by this philosopher by using the nonsense word (*) “quus” (“kwoos”). That book was titled “[this philosopher] on Rules and Private Language” and was by Saul Kripke. Reflecting a critique by Frank P. Ramsey, the only published academic paper by this philosopher was titled “Some Remarks on Logical Form.” This philosopher illustrated that private languages are incoherent with his “beetle-in-a-box” thought experiment. For 10 points, name this Austrian philosopher who wrote Philosophical Investigations.
69fe677ae2442def495d8607.question_sanitized: As part of her work on artificial intelligence, Margaret Masterman was the first to introduce this philosopher's work into computational linguistics. Colour-coded notebooks of lectures by this philosopher were later published as the Blue and Brown Books. In a 1982 work, another philosopher explained a "sceptical problem" introduced by this philosopher by using the nonsense word (*) "quus" ("kwoos"). That book was titled "[this philosopher] on Rules and Private Language" and was by Saul Kripke. Reflecting a critique by Frank P. Ramsey, the only published academic paper by this philosopher was titled "Some Remarks on Logical Form." This philosopher illustrated that private languages are incoherent with his "beetle-in-a-box" thought experiment. For 10 points, name this Austrian philosopher who wrote Philosophical Investigations.
69fe677ae2442def495d8607.answer_sanitized: Ludwig Wittgenstein [or Ludwig Josef Johann Wittgenstein]

69fe677ae2442def495d860d.leadin: This thinker proposed the idea of “atomic swerve”, which can determine free will for living things. For 10 points each:
69fe677ae2442def495d860d.leadin_sanitized: This thinker proposed the idea of "atomic swerve", which can determine free will for living things. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this hedonistic thinker and follower of Democritus. This thinker’s namesake school of philosophy declares that pleasure and the absence of pain and fear should be humanity’s intrinsic goal.
Epicureanism is often contrasted with this other school of philosophy that had adherents such as Zeno of Citium and Marcus Aurelius, and which sees virtue and self-control as essential in life.

Note to players: original language term required. Epicureanism believes that freeing yourself from mental disturbances can put you in a state of mind known by this term. Roughly translated as “tranquility”, this term is often contrasted with aponia.
parts_sanitized: Name this hedonistic thinker and follower of Democritus. This thinker's namesake school of philosophy declares that pleasure and the absence of pain and fear should be humanity's intrinsic goal.
Epicureanism is often contrasted with this other school of philosophy that had adherents such as Zeno of Citium and Marcus Aurelius, and which sees virtue and self-control as essential in life.

Note to players: original language term required. Epicureanism believes that freeing yourself from mental disturbances can put you in a state of mind known by this term. Roughly translated as "tranquility", this term is often contrasted with aponia.

69fe677ae2442def495d861c.leadin: This opera contains sections named for its title character “killing Marie” and “drowning”. For 10 points each:
69fe677ae2442def495d861c.leadin_sanitized: This opera contains sections named for its title character "killing Marie" and "drowning". For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this first opera composed by Alban Berg. This modernist opera follows the titular soldier succumbing to madness in the face of militaristic oppression.
Alban Berg was a member of the Second School of this city. Other members of that school named for this central European city include Arnold Schoenberg and Anton Webern.

Inspired by the Second Viennese School, this composer conducted the 3-act completed premiere of Berg’s second opera Lulu. This composer also created an abstract chamber cantata that sets poetry by René Char (“SHAR”) and used symbolist sonnets for his piece Pli selon pli.
parts_sanitized: Identify this first opera composed by Alban Berg. This modernist opera follows the titular soldier succumbing to madness in the face of militaristic oppression.
Alban Berg was a member of the Second School of this city. Other members of that school named for this central European city include Arnold Schoenberg and Anton Webern.

Inspired by the Second Viennese School, this composer conducted the 3-act completed premiere of Berg's second opera Lulu. This composer also created an abstract chamber cantata that sets poetry by Rene Char ("SHAR") and used symbolist sonnets for his piece Pli selon pli.
answers: Pierre Boulez(The cantata is Le Marteau sans maître or The Hammer Without A Master)
answers_sanitized: Vienna [or Wien, accept Second Viennese School]
Pierre Boulez(The cantata is Le Marteau sans maitre or The Hammer Without A Master)

In On the Basis of Morality, Schopenhauer criticised this Kantian concept for ultimately being based on selfish egoism. This ethical principle states that behaviour should be in accordance with a universal law.

69fe677fe2442def495d8652.question: In a short story by this author, Sykes dies after being bitten by a rattlesnake he intended to poison his wife Delia. Joe Kandy’s ghost haunts the title character of another story by this author, who dies when he accidentally steps in front of a buzzsaw. This author of “Sweat” and “Spunk”, who wrote a novel narrated by its protagonist to Phoeby Watson, was accused of plagiarising Emma Roche in a non-fiction work about the last survivor of the (*) Clotilda, Cudjoe Lewis. This author, who began one novel with the line “Ships at a distance have every man’s wish on board”, was the subject of an Alice Walker essay about “looking” for her. After unsuccessful marriages with Logan Killicks and Jody Starks, the protagonist of a novel by this author is forced to shoot her lover Tea Cake after he is bitten by a rabid dog. For 10 points, name this author who depicted Florida’s Black communities in novels like Their Eyes Were Watching God.
